title: Assistive Technology for Blind People
subtitle: Smart Navigation Assistance using Ultrasonic Sensor & Buzzer
desc: Detect obstacles in real-time and alert visually impaired users via buzzer feedback.
fullDesc: Build an intelligent assistive device designed to help blind individuals navigate safely. The system uses ultrasonic waves to detect obstacles in front of the user and provides instant vibrating buzzer feedback.
author: Shaheena Rai
innovation: Low-cost, lightweight smart attachment for walking sticks to prevent collisions for visually impaired individuals.
problemStatement: Visually impaired people face high risks of injury from unexpected obstacles that traditional walking sticks cannot detect easily.
solutionApproach: Used an HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor with an Arduino UNO to measure obstacle distance. The system triggers a vibrating buzzer sound that alerts the user as an obstacle gets closer.
guide: Connect Ultrasonic Sensor: VCC to Arduino 5V, GND to GND, Trig to D2, and Echo to D3
Connect Buzzer positive pin to Arduino D4 and negative pin to GND
Power the Arduino UNO using a 9V battery or USB cable
Open Arduino IDE and write code to calculate distance using the ultrasonic sensor
Set the logic: If an obstacle is detected within the set range, activate the buzzer to vibrate/sound an alert
Upload the code to your Arduino UNO and test it with various obstacles

youtubeVideos.title: Smart Assistive Device Demo
youtubeVideos.desc: Demonstrating the completed project by Shaheena Rai

achievements: Designed for social impact and accessibility
